findings.description: When visible=0, value[-visible:] becomes value[-0:] which equals value[0:] (the entire string), so the full input is appended after the mask instead of zero trailing chars.
The visible=0 slicing defect causes mask_middle to emit the full unmasked secret, defeating redaction and disclosing sensitive values.
Short-value branch returns mask_char*len(value), leaking the exact length of the masked secret despite the docstring claiming it avoids leaking content.
Negative max_length makes slug[:max_length] slice from the end (e.g. -1 drops the last char) instead of raising, silently corrupting the slug.
levenshtein is O(len(a)*len(b)) with no length guard, so two large untrusted strings can cause a quadratic-time resource exhaustion (DoS).
All-non-ASCII input (e.g. pure emoji/CJK) yields an empty slug with no fallback, which can produce empty or colliding identifiers for callers.
collapse_whitespace (and other helpers) assume non-null str and raise AttributeError on None input with no validation per the documented contract.
indent_block uses splitlines(), silently dropping a trailing newline so round-tripping text loses the final line terminator.
Negative spaces in indent_block produces an empty pad (no-op) instead of raising, silently ignoring an invalid argument.
